root_text.sn56.19:1.2: Tattha aparimāṇā vaṇṇā aparimāṇā byañjanā aparimāṇā saṅkāsanā:
root_text.sn56.19:1.6: ‘idaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā ariyasaccan’ti, bhikkhave, mayā paññattaṁ.
root_text.sn56.19:1.7: Tattha aparimāṇā vaṇṇā aparimāṇā byañjanā aparimāṇā saṅkāsanā:
root_text.sn56.19:2.1: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yogo karaṇīyo …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yogo karaṇīyo”ti.

translation_text.sn56.19:0.2: 2. Rolling Forth the Wheel of Dhamma
translation_text.sn56.19:1.1: “Mendicants, I’ve declared: ‘This is the noble truth of suffering.’
translation_text.sn56.19:1.2: And there are limitless explanations, phrases, and expressions about that:
translation_text.sn56.19:1.3: ‘This is another way of saying that this is the noble truth of suffering.’
translation_text.sn56.19:1.4: I’ve declared: ‘This is the noble truth of the origin of suffering.’ …
translation_text.sn56.19:1.5: I’ve declared: ‘This is the noble truth of the cessation of suffering.’ …
translation_text.sn56.19:1.6: I’ve declared: ‘This is the noble truth of the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering.’
translation_text.sn56.19:1.7: And there are limitless explanations, phrases, and expressions about that:
translation_text.sn56.19:1.8: ‘This is another way of saying that this is the noble truth of the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering.’
translation_text.sn56.19:2.1: That’s why you should practice meditation …”
